exemplar.title: Does acquiring knowledge destroy our sense of wonder? Discuss with reference to two areas of knowledge.

detailedExemplarData.title: Does acquiring knowledge destroy our sense of wonder? Discuss with reference to two areas of knowledge.

strengths: Sustained focus on the prescribed title with clear linkage to two areas of knowledge (human sciences and natural sciences).
Well-chosen illustrative examples (e.g., Henry Molaison case study, Maguireâs taxi-driver study, vaccine immunology, enzyme catalysis) that demonstrate understanding of disciplinary concepts.
Effective structuring of arguments and counterclaims, signaled by transitional phrases such as âOn the other hand.â
Conceptual clarity through careful definition of key terms (âacquiring knowledge,â âsense of wonderâ) which frames the subsequent discussion.
Interdisciplinary synthesis in conclusion underlining how psychology and biology jointly inform our sense of wonder.
weaknesses: Analysis often remains descriptive and occasionally repetitive, with limited critical depth into why or under what conditions knowledge deepens or diminishes wonder.
Several long, compound sentences and informal phrasing undermine conciseness and academic tone; more precise, active-voice constructions are needed.
Links between specific examples and the central knowledge question are not always fully developed; implications and nuances are only partially explored.
Structural and citation issues: incomplete sentence fragments, redundancy in counterclaims, and lack of proper referencing of primary research (e.g., HM studies).
Conclusion restates earlier points without offering new insights or broader forward-looking implications.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay meets the descriptors for the 5â6 band by maintaining a sustained focus on the title, making effective links to two areas of knowledge, and supporting clear arguments with specific examples. There is some evaluation of differing perspectives, though this evaluation is more asserted than deeply examined, and the analysis often lapses into description. The structural and stylistic weaknessesâlengthy sentences, informal tone, occasional redundancy, and lack of citationâlimit the overall analytical depth. These strengths and limitations together justify the official examinerâs allocation of 6 marks: a solid performance with clear focus and relevant examples, yet lacking the consistently critical evaluation and nuanced exploration required for higher bands.

annotations.comment: The thesis explicitly identifies two areas of knowledgeâhuman sciences and natural sciencesâdemonstrating a focused response to the prescribed title.
The transition to a counterclaim with âOn the other handâ is effective in signaling a shift in perspective and structuring the argument clearly.
This sentence fragment introduces the counterclaim but is incomplete; ensure each paragraph starts with a full, coherent statement.
Using a personal vaccine anecdote engages the reader and shows how knowledge can both demystify and inspire awe in biology.
The introduction relies on broad generalizations about wonder without articulating a precise knowledge question to guide the analysis; consider formulating a clear KQ.
Several sentences are lengthy and compound, which undermines the conciseness of argument; break them into clearer, more focused statements.
The student clearly defines key terms âacquiring knowledgeâ and âsense of wonder,â which establishes conceptual clarity and frames the subsequent discussion effectively.
Consider linking examples introduced here directly to implications for the sense of wonder to strengthen coherence between concepts and evidence.
The use of Henry Molaison (HM) as a case study effectively illustrates how localization theory can raise new questions and deepen wonder about memory and consciousness.
The analysis of HMâs case remains largely descriptive; the student should critically examine why this discovery shifts our understanding rather than merely restating its impact.
The mention of the âdeja vu momentâ is intriguing but underdeveloped; explain its relevance to ongoing neuroscience debates to add analytical depth.
Highlighting ongoing unknowns in consciousness research showcases an awareness of the evolving nature of knowledge, which enriches the argument.
There is no citation or reference to original research on HM; include proper acknowledgment of sources to strengthen academic rigour.
Incorporating Maguireâs taxiâdriver study effectively grounds the counterclaim in a concrete example from neuroscience.
The detailed account of enzyme activity and factors affecting function demonstrates a solid understanding of natural sciences content.
This paragraph relies on passive constructions; recast key sentences in active voice to enhance clarity and engagement.
Explicitly link the enzyme example back to the title by discussing how biochemical predictability can both diminish and spark wonder.
The counterclaim discussion reiterates earlier points about mechanical brain function without adding new analysis; introduce additional nuance or evidence.
Statements are overly deterministic (e.g., âpredictable biological responsesâ); include modal qualifiers to acknowledge uncertainty and complexity.
The overgeneralization about predictability undermines depth; consider discussing conditions under which predictability might itself evoke wonder.
The analysis of Maguireâs study is surfaceâlevel; explain how knowing about structural brain changes specifically influences our sense of wonder.
The conclusion closely mirrors the introduction without offering new insights or broader implications; consider adding a forwardâlooking perspective.
Integration of psychology and biology in the final paragraph underlines the interdisciplinary nature of the discussion effectively.
The immunological explanation is accurate but simplified; deepen the analysis by discussing the role of memory B cells at a molecular level.
Phrases like âas turns outâ are informal; revise for academic tone to maintain consistency throughout the essay.
Consider introducing a contrasting biology example beyond immunology to broaden the natural sciences discussion.
Consider ending with a compelling example or question that invites the reader to reflect on ongoing knowledge frontiers and wonder.
Identifying temperature, pH, and substrate concentration as variables shows depth of analysis and supports nuanced reflection on complexity.
The biology counterclaim repeats earlier vaccine points, creating redundancy; restructure to avoid repetition and introduce fresh insight.
There is a logical inconsistency in asserting that vaccines both enrich and diminish wonder without reconciling these perspectives; clarify this tension.
Organize biology claims and counterclaims in separate paragraphs to improve structural clarity and reader navigation.
The conclusion succinctly restates the central claim that knowledge transforms and enriches wonder, reinforcing the essayâs coherence.
The conclusion misses an opportunity to suggest how future knowledge advancements might further influence our sense of wonder.

The essay does not
reach a standard
described by levels
1â5 or is not a
response to one of
the prescribed titles
on the list for the
current session.
The discussion is weakly connected to the title. While there may be links to the areas of knowledge, any relevant points are descriptive or consist only of unsupported assertions.

The discussion is connected to the title and makes superficial or limited links to areas of knowledge. The discussion is largely descriptive. Limited arguments are offered but they are unclear and are not supported by effective examples.

The discussion
is focused on
the title and is
developed with
some links to
areas of
knowledge.
Arguments are
offered and are
supported by
examples.
There is some
awareness of
different points
of view.

The discussion is focused on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and supported by examples. There is awareness and some evaluation of different points of view.

The discussion has a sustained focus on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and effectively supported by specific examples. The implications of arguments are considered. There is clear awareness and evaluation of different points of view.
